unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * * RFC2195 CRAM-MD5 authentication * RFC2617 Basic and Digest Access Authentication * RFC2831 DIGEST-MD5 authentication * RFC4422 Simple Authentication and Security Layer (SASL) * RFC4616 PLAIN authentication * RFC5802 SCRAM-SHA-1 authentication * RFC7677 SCRAM-SHA-256 authentication * RFC6749 OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework * RFC7628 A Set of SASL Mechanisms for OAuth * Draft LOGIN SASL Mechanism <draft-murchison-sasl-login-00.txt> * ***************************************************************************/
/* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */

/* * Curl_sasl_cleanup() * * This is used to cleanup any libraries or curl modules used by the sasl * functions. * * Parameters: * * conn [in] - The connection data. * authused [in] - The authentication mechanism used. */

function.name: Curl_sasl_cleanup

unit.comment: /* * Curl_sasl_decode_mech() * * Convert a SASL mechanism name into a token. * * Parameters: * * ptr [in] - The mechanism string. * maxlen [in] - Maximum mechanism string length. * len [out] - If not NULL, effective name length. * * Returns the SASL mechanism token or 0 if no match. */

function.name: Curl_sasl_decode_mech

unit.comment: /* * Curl_sasl_parse_url_auth_option() * * Parse the URL login options. */

function.name: Curl_sasl_parse_url_auth_option
Curl_sasl_init
function.parameter_list: (struct SASL *sasl, struct Curl_easy *data, const struct SASLproto *params)

unit.comment: /* * sasl_state() * * This is the ONLY way to change SASL state! */

function.name: sasl_state

unit.comment: /* Get the SASL server message and convert it to binary. */

function.name: get_server_message
build_message

unit.comment: /* * Curl_sasl_can_authenticate() * * Check if we have enough auth data and capabilities to authenticate. */

function.name: Curl_sasl_can_authenticate

unit.comment: /* * Curl_sasl_start() * * Calculate the required login details for SASL authentication. */

function.name: Curl_sasl_start
function.parameter_list: (struct SASL *sasl, struct Curl_easy *data, bool force_ir, saslprogress *progress)

function.name: Curl_sasl_continue
function.parameter_list: (struct SASL *sasl, struct Curl_easy *data, int code, saslprogress *progress)

unit.comment: /* protocols are enabled that use SASL */
